headline: Sand Mining Removes 78 Million Tonnes from Johor Rivers
context: DID Johor reported that 78 million tonnes of river sand were extracted in 2024, contributing to accelerated riverbank erosion affecting 14 kampungs along Sungai Johor.

cards.big: Sand is Malaysia's second most extracted natural resource after palm oil.
cards.sub: 78 million tonnes left Johor's rivers in 2024 — most exported to Singapore's land reclamation projects.

cards.big: 14 kampungs along Sungai Johor report accelerating erosion.
cards.sub: Riverbanks retreated an average of 3.2 metres in 2024. Five homes were declared unsafe.

cards.big: Sand export revenue was RM890M in 2024.
cards.sub: Erosion repair costs borne by DID exceeded RM340M — effectively subsidising foreign reclamation.

cards.big: Selangor banned river sand mining in 2019.
cards.sub: Production shifted entirely to Johor and Perak, where regulations remain weaker.

cards.big: Selling sand at RM890M while spending RM340M on erosion repair is a net loss disguised as revenue.
A national sand policy is overdue. State-by-state regulation creates a race to the bottom where the weakest rules attract the most extraction.
